Micallef Tonight was an Australian late-night variety show that aired on the Nine Network in 2003. Hosted by comedian Shaun Micallef, the program was known for its unconventional approach to the traditional talk-show format. Each episode featured celebrity interviews, musical performances, and recurring segments like âShaun on his High Horse,â where Micallef humorously critiqued pop culture while perched on a gymnastâs vaulting horse. The show often employed surreal humor and absurdist sketches, such as introducing guests with elaborate monologues only to dismiss them abruptly, or requesting performers to insert random words into their songs to prove they werenât lip-syncing. Despite its innovative style, *Micallef Tonight* was canceled after 13 episodes due to declining ratings and controversies over certain segments, including a parody that reportedly angered network owner Kerry Packer.

Despite its short run, *Micallef Tonight* garnered critical acclaim and developed a cult following. The show won the ARIA Award for Best Comedy Release in 2008 for its DVD compilation, *The Expurgated Micallef Tonight: The Very Best of Shaun Micallefâs Short-Lived but Brilliant Tonight Show*. This release included unaired material and a commentary track featuring Micallef and guest Tony Martin. The series is often remembered for its witty, subversive humor and remains a significant part of Shaun Micallefâs comedic legacy.

messages.content: I'm making the very first part. :)
So it is an entire modular system on it's own, combining all of the things that make up a synth.
Then, a VCO Mixer, FM Modulation, VCF (Voltage Controlled Filter), Envelope Generator, etc...
They have 2 x AS3340's in the K2
Voltage Controlled Oscillator 1 and Voltage Controlled Oscillator 2.
On the left hand side of that image, you see two columns.

Here's a similar Eurorack format AS3340 module built by a company called Doboz. But I'm making mine from scratch.

https://youtu.be/mkRFhSnZLvM

The AS3340 is the same chip in your Behringer K2 (edited)
The AS3340 is the same chip in our Behringer K2
Thatâs my design based on Eddy Bergmanâs DIGISOUND 80.
I just have to order the PCB
Combine it with all this other stuffâ¦ and you get a VCO.
You connect it up to jacks and pots and it becomes a Voltage Controlled Oscillator.
AS3340 is a famous really old IC chip made by CES which has been remade by AlfaZeta. Itâs a square/sin/triangle/fm generator in a tiny CIF16 pin IC format.

Follow @entreprneurinsights for more insights like this

Potato Parcel is a quirky and unique service that allows people to send custom messages or images printed on potatoes, making for a humorous and memorable gift.

Founded in 2015, it gained widespread attention after appearing on Shark Tank, where entrepreneur Mark Cuban invested in the business. 

The concept is simple yet entertaining: users choose a potato, add their personalized message or photo, and the company delivers it to the recipient.

Potato Parcel has become a viral sensation, proving that even the most unconventional ideas can find success in niche markets.

Credits: @potatoparcel @usatoday

share.share_text:
Youâre going to get what you deserve.
10.10.25
Bob got it tonight

Weâre in a Cosmic Dream. Things are a little bit insane right now: the debate this week has revealed the laughable inadequacy of our political system, the southern states are bringing in the Ten Commandments and Bible study to public school, Gaza is an unbearable apocalypse. I am sending peace for all of the above and I also would like to remind everyone that this is a dream. 

All the mystics across the world religious traditions say this. From Rumi to Laozi to every Zen master and too many Hindus to name, to even Jesus in the Gnostic literature.

And one of my favorite pieces of evidence from the past few years that we, as young Alice, fell down a cosmic rabbithole, is two words: Brady Feigl.

Most people think about working out as a way to improve their body composition or get strong, but they often forget about the health benefits.

These MRI scans are a good example of how living an active lifestyle can help preserve muscle mass and bone density as you age.

Why is this important? Well, more muscle and better bone density means being stronger, more durable, and more independent.

In other words, when it comes to muscle mass, either you use it or you lose it.
